title: C4OS Grill Question 003: Agent Authority
description: This question defines the authority model objectively: what users can do freely, what agents can do by default, and what requires permission. Submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat.

questions.label: What can an agent do with files inside the active project folder?
options: Read only by default; writes require approval
Read and write freely when the user's request implies file work
Read freely; write freely only if Git/revert exists
Ask every time before reading or writing
questions.help: This is about files inside the active trusted project. User browsing authority is separate.

questions.label: What can an agent do outside the active project folder?
options: No access
Read only with explicit permission
Read and write with explicit permission
Use previously granted chat permission for matching paths/actions
questions.help: Your prior answer says outside-project access should require permission unless previously allowed in chat.

questions.label: What public web browsing can an agent do?
options: Any public website when it falls within the user's request
Any public website only after per-domain approval
Only localhost and documentation sites
No agent web browsing in MVP
questions.help: This excludes private logged-in actions unless you explicitly include them in the notes.

questions.label: Can the agent use logged-in Browser sessions?
options: No, user-only
Yes, if the user's request clearly requires it
Yes, but only after approval per site/session
Post-MVP only
questions.help: This is separate from the user being able to log in and browse freely.

questions.label: What should C4OS record for agent actions?
options: File reads inside project
File writes inside project
Outside-project permission grants
Public websites visited by agent
Logged-in sites used by agent
No audit beyond chat transcript
questions.help: Select the action records future users should be able to review.

questions.label: Clarifications or corrections
questions.help: Add any nuance about Git, revert responsibility, prior permission in chat, public vs private websites, or what counts as being within the user's request.
